gene_region.label: TPMT transcribed interval
gene_region.definition: Canonical TPMT genomic interval on GRCh37 / hg19 from Ensembl GRCh37 lookup, aligned to the hg19 coordinate system used by this app.

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window relative to the reverse-strand transcription start, used to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: TPMT encodes thiopurine S-methyltransferase, a thiopurine inactivation enzyme. This bundle emphasizes thiopurine methylation, active nucleotide accumulation, myelosuppression risk, and immunosuppressant pharmacogenomics.
gene_context.clinical_context: The local TPMT bundle is thiopurine pharmacogenomics oriented. Interpret markers through exact allele class, zygosity, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context rather than as stand-alone deterministic calls.
variant_effect_overview: TPMT variants can alter or tag thiopurine methylation, active nucleotide accumulation, myelosuppression risk, and immunosuppressant pharmacogenomics; effect direction depends on allele class, tissue, dosage, and biological context.
Common markers, pharmacogenetic alleles, structural events, and high-impact pathogenic variants should be kept separate during interpretation.
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: TPMT methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a thiopurine methylation, active nucleotide accumulation, myelosuppression risk, and immunosuppressant pharmacogenomics gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
methylation_effects: Promoter or first-exon methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local expression state for TPMT.
Because TPMT biology is tissue and pathway dependent, methylation values are context rather than a stand-alone biomarker.

Combined sequence plus methylation findings are most useful when interpreted against thiopurine methylation, active nucleotide accumulation, myelosuppression risk, and immunosuppressant pharmacogenomics.
methylation_condition_research: Epigenetic regulation studies that use TPMT promoter or gene-body methylation as expression-context evidence.
Variant-plus-methylation review for thiopurine methylation, active nucleotide accumulation, myelosuppression risk, and immunosuppressant pharmacogenomics.

evidence.label: Ensembl GRCh37 ENSG00000137364: TPMT coordinate source

biorender_visuals.label: TPMT biochemical figure starter
biorender_visuals.focus: TPMT: thiopurine methylation, active nucleotide accumulation, myelosuppression risk, and immunosuppressant pharmacogenomics
biorender_visuals.usage_note: BioRender template and icon-library search results are bundled as figure starters. Open the template link in BioRender, then add or replace the listed icons to make a gene-specific biochemical pathway image.

gene_context.concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a TPMT thiopurine pharmacogenomics thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to thiopurine methylation, active nucleotide accumulation, myelosuppression risk, and immunosuppressant pharmacogenomics and TPMT thiopurine toxicity context, Mercaptopurine/azathioprine dosing biology. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support.

variant_records.clinical_interpretation: rs1800460 (TPMT*3B) is curated as a TPMT marker for TPMT thiopurine toxicity context, Mercaptopurine/azathioprine dosing biology. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the thiopurine methylation, active nucleotide accumulation, myelosuppression risk, and immunosuppressant pharmacogenomics pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_records.clinical_significance: Curated research and clinical-context marker; external clinical classification is required for diagnostic use.
functional_effects: Can modify or tag thiopurine methylation, active nucleotide accumulation, myelosuppression risk, and immunosuppressant pharmacogenomics.
Best interpreted with tissue, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context.
research_context: Use this marker as a TPMT pathway-context signal, not as a stand-alone diagnosis.
Sequence, methylation, expression, and protein data may point in different directions and should be reconciled.
variant_records.methylation_interpretation: Pair rs1800460 (TPMT*3B) with TPMT methylation only as local regulatory context; methylation does not prove the variant's biochemical effect.

literature_findings.genotypes: rs1800460 (TPMT*3B) or linked TPMT locus genotype states
literature_findings.phenotype: TPMT thiopurine toxicity context, Mercaptopurine/azathioprine dosing biology
literature_findings.finding: The local curation treats rs1800460 (TPMT*3B) as a gene-specific marker for TPMT thiopurine toxicity context, Mercaptopurine/azathioprine dosing biology and thiopurine methylation, active nucleotide accumulation, myelosuppression risk, and immunosuppressant pharmacogenomics.
